description:
Advances: specled.verification, specled.tagged_tests

Deliverable:
Agent-legibility fix for the failure mode that produced s65.3: a single timed-out merged run rendered as '59 verification_command_failed findings', which pattern-matches for an LLM to 'the codebase is broken'. Two changes, same emission site:

1. Preserve the timeout fact instead of erasing it. lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex run_command (~line 1897) currently coerces :timeout to exit_code 1, making a hung/slow command indistinguishable from failing tests. Carry a timed_out marker on the result map and emit a DISTINCT finding code verification_command_timeout (instead of verification_command_failed) when it fires. Message must carry the remedy inline, e.g.: "command exceeded <N>ms (verification.command_timeout_ms in .spec/config.yml, default 120000); tests were not observed to fail. Re-run with --command-timeout-ms <2N> to confirm this is a budget problem." Non-timeout failures keep verification_command_failed unchanged.
2. Say the fate is shared. Findings produced from the merged tagged_tests result (one command distributed to every participating subject, verifier.ex ~1847) must state event multiplicity in each per-subject message, e.g.: "aggregated tagged_tests run (1 command shared by <N> subjects) — this finding reflects the shared run, not a subject-specific failure." Applies to both the timeout and failure variants of the merged-run finding.

Spec work (this is spec-governed behavior):
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md: REVISE specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced — statement currently says a non-zero exit code shall be recorded on timeout; revise to: the timeout shall be recorded distinctly on the result and surfaced as verification_command_timeout, while still marking the verification failed for gating purposes. Note: the same requirement's 'the spawned process shall be killed' clause is aspirational today (Port.close does not kill the child) — do NOT attempt to fix process reaping here; it is stage 1 of the streaming-attribution epic. Add requirement + scenario for the new finding code.
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md: new must requirement + scenario for shared-fate message context on merged-run findings. Do NOT touch specled.tagged_tests.merged_run_attribution semantics — the single result is still distributed to all entries; only the message content changes.
- Author a short .spec/decisions/ entry only if the requirement revision is judged architecturally significant; otherwise skip.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check.

Depends on specled_-s65.6 (the remediation message references --command-timeout-ms; land the flag first).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: additions to test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs — timeout produces verification_command_timeout (not _failed) with budget + remediation in message; non-timeout non-zero exit still produces verification_command_failed; merged-run findings carry the shared-by-N context in every participating subject's finding; N matches the participating entry count. Tag with the new/revised requirement ids.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: the scenarios authored in this ticket.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/** (new file only, if warranted)
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- No process-group kill / orphan reaping (streaming-attribution epic stage 1).
- No per-subject attribution changes — one merged result, distributed to all, exactly as today.
- No new severity semantics: verification_command_timeout gates the same as verification_command_failed (fails the run); only the code and message differ.
- No formatter, no artifact, no resume logic.
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers; escape interpolation in doc strings.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket
title: Timeout-distinct finding + shared-fate context in merged tagged_tests messages
